subtitle: Front, back or inner thigh — location not pinpointed

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: General thigh pain often reflects accumulated fatigue from mileage increases

mild.description: General thigh fatigue or aching after running
mild.can_run_note: Yes — easy pace, try to identify which part of the thigh aches most
why: General muscle fatigue from training load
Multiple muscles fatiguing simultaneously in long efforts
Delayed onset soreness from harder sessions
relief: Foam roll entire thigh — front, back and outer
Easy cycling or swimming to flush out fatigue
Protein and carbohydrate recovery nutrition within 30 min of running
prevention: Use this app to identify the specific structure and get targeted advice
Balanced strength programme covering quads, hamstrings and adductors
Avoid large mileage jumps week to week

moderate.description: Consistent thigh pain affecting running form or pace
moderate.can_run_note: Easy runs only — try to locate the pain more precisely
why: Specific muscle strain or tendinopathy not yet identified
Referred pain from hip or knee
Fatigue accumulation from consecutive hard days
relief: Reduce mileage by 40% and eliminate speed work
Try to identify whether front, back or inner thigh is the source
prevention: Rest week every 4th week of training

severe.can_run_note: Do not run — seek proper diagnosis
why: Possible stress fracture of the femur — needs imaging
relief: Imaging if pain is localised and severe
prevention: Return to running only with a clear diagnosis and plan

see_physio_if: Deep bone-like pain rather than muscle ache
Pain at rest or at night
Swelling or bruising on the thigh
No improvement after 5 days of rest
